steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: The blank contains an appositive — carbon-13 followed by its abbreviation (13C) — that renames a rare carbon isotope. An appositive that interrupts a sentence must be set off from what follows it with a comma. The blank must not place a comma inside the appositive between the name and its abbreviation, which would break the unit apart.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each option against the rule that the appositive must remain a single unit and be followed by a comma:
  • Choice A — ‘carbon-13, (13C)’: Places a comma between carbon-13 and its abbreviation, incorrectly splitting the appositive. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘carbon-13 (13C)’: Keeps the appositive intact but lacks the closing comma needed to separate it from the rest of the sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘carbon-13, (13C),’: Splits the appositive with an internal comma and also adds a closing comma — doubly wrong. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘carbon-13 (13C),’: Keeps carbon-13 (13C) as one unit and correctly places a comma after it to separate it from the following clause. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
